subjects.name: Electronic Devices and Circuits
subjects.description: Core electronic devices, circuits, and exam-oriented fundamentals.

subjects.name: Database Management Systems
subjects.description: Relational database design, SQL, and query optimization basics.

topics.title: Diode Basics
topics.summary: Understand diode construction, biasing, and V-I characteristics.

topics.title: Rectifiers
topics.summary: Study half-wave and full-wave rectifiers with ripple concepts.

topics.title: Transistor Basics
topics.summary: Cover transistor operation, regions, and common configurations.

topics.title: SQL Basics
topics.summary: Learn core SQL queries, selection, projection, and filtering.

topics.title: Joins
topics.summary: Understand inner, outer, and self joins with practical examples.

topics.title: Normalization
topics.summary: Study normal forms, anomalies, and schema refinement.

examContexts.title: JNTU R22 EDC
examContexts.description: Exam-focused EDC preparation aligned with JNTU R22 patterns.

examContexts.title: JNTU R22 DBMS
examContexts.description: Exam-focused DBMS preparation aligned with JNTU R22 patterns.

questions.prompt: A diode conducts heavily when it is in which bias condition?
questions.explanation: A pn-junction diode allows significant current in forward bias.
Half-wave and full-wave rectifiers are the two standard forms.

questions.prompt: Which SQL clause is used to filter rows?
questions.explanation: WHERE filters rows before grouping and ordering.

questions.prompt: List two join types used in relational queries.
questions.explanation: Inner join and left join are standard join types in SQL.

questions.prompt: What problem does normalization help reduce in database design?
questions.explanation: Normalization reduces redundancy and update anomalies.
